zh.Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德
zh.Fonthill Gifford, Salisbury, Wiltshire,: 英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Giffard於大約1122年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里時，他的父親Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield23歲，母親Berthe fitzPons17歲。他和Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke至少有1個女兒。他去世於1232年在英國奧可福爾享年111歲。

zh-hans.Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德
zh-hans.Fonthill Gifford, Salisbury, Wiltshire,: 英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Giffard于大约1122年出生在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里时，他的父亲Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield23岁，母亲Berthe fitzPons17岁。他和Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke至少有1个女儿。他去世于1232年在英国奧可福爾享年111岁。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Giffard was born about 1122, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield, was 23 and his mother, Berthe fitzPons, was 17. He had at least 1 daughter with Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. He died in 1232, in Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 111.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Giffard est né environ 1122, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Berthe fitzPons, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. Il est décédé en 1232, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 111 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Giffard um 1122 in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield 23 und seine Mutter Berthe fitzPons 17. Er hatte mit Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1232 im Alter von 111 Jahren in Oxfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Giffard è nato il circa 1122 nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Berthe fitzPons, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. Morì nel 1232, nel Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 111 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Giffardが約1122年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで生まれた時，父Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfieldは23歳，母Berthe fitzPonsは17歳であった。彼はAlice FitzHugh of Greystokeとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1232年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで111歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Giffard(이)가 대략 1122년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Berthe fitzPons(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1232년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 111세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Giffard nasceu aproximadamente 1122, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Berthe fitzPons, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. Ele faleceu em 1232, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 111 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Giffard родился около 1122 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Berthe fitzPons, 17. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. Он умер в 1232 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 111 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Giffard nació aproximadamente 1122, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Berthe fitzPons, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Alice FitzHugh of Greystoke. Murió en 1232, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 111 años.
